descriptionInUI: Runtime for the enemy comms-jamming feature. Inert unless the mission generator emits dcsRetribution.commsJam (the 'Enemy comms jamming' setting is on, an enemy IADS comms/command-center node is alive, and blue has briefed channels). While at least one emitted C2 node is alive, the plugin transmits duty-cycled barrage noise (trigger.action.radioTransmission) on a rotating subset of the briefed blue channels from the node's map position -- real power/distance falloff, so the interference is worst deep in enemy territory and SRS users hear it through their cockpit-tuned radios. With the intel gate on (the 'needs captured aircrew' setting, default), the jammer stays dormant until red actually holds a captured pilot's comms plan: a POW held from an earlier turn compromises the net from mission start, and a pilot captured live (the Combat SAR capture race) compromises it after a short exploitation delay -- rescue your people and the net stays clean. GUARD and ATC are never on the target list (filtered in Python by construction), and the kneeboard carries a JAM BACKUP channel the jammer never touches. Audio pressure ONLY: no force-model change, the plugin owns no kills -- destroying the C2 node (an ordinary IADS strike target, recorded natively) is what silences the jamming. Bursts begin only after a startup grace so nobody is jammed mid-alignment.

specificOptions.nameInUI: Burst length: noise transmission per jammed channel (s)
Max channels jammed at all: caps the total distinct briefed freqs stepped on, highest-priority first (human flights, then AWACS). 10 = every briefed channel.
Channels stepped on per burst cycle
Transmitter power (W) -- range AND strength of the interference (DCS models the falloff; higher = louder and reaches further from the node)
Startup grace before the first burst (s)
Exploitation delay: live capture to first burst (s)
